name: callout
title: Callout
description: A permanent, non-dismissible inline notification for contextual information that stays on the page. Use for static advisories inside a panel, form, or page section ‚Äî not for transient feedback.

meta.docs:
# Callout

A permanent, non-dismissible inline notification for contextual information that stays on the page. Use for static advisories inside a panel, form, or page section ‚Äî not for transient feedback.

## When to use

- **Contextual info** ("Heads up ‚Äî this affects 12 records") ‚Äî use default `<Alert>` + `<Info />` icon and an `<AlertTitle>` / `<AlertDescription>`.
- **Static warning** ("Some changes cannot be undone") ‚Äî `<Alert className="border-yellow-500/50 text-yellow-700 [&>svg]:text-yellow-600">` + `<AlertTriangle />`.
- **Quiet upsell or hint with a CTA** ‚Äî compose `Alert` + a low-emphasis `<Button variant="outline" size="sm">` placed inside.
- **Subtle/low-contrast notice** ‚Äî set `className="border-transparent bg-muted"` on the `Alert` to mute the visual weight.

## When not to use

- **Dismissible advisory or transient feedback** ‚Äî use a `Sonner` toast.
- **Action-required banner with a primary CTA and dismiss button** ‚Äî use the `ActionableNotification` migration pattern (`Alert` + `Button` + your own dismiss state).
- **Field-level error message** ‚Äî use the form field's inline error, not an Alert.
- **Blocking confirmation** ‚Äî use `AlertDialog`.

## Composition

- Carbon's `Callout` is monolithic; the shadcn `Alert` is decomposed. Render the icon, `AlertTitle`, `AlertDescription`, and (optionally) a `Button` as children ‚Äî the layout is yours.
- Status colours beyond `default` and `destructive` (info, warning, success) are not built in. Apply Tailwind classes per usage, or extend `alertVariants` with named variants if you reuse the same status across the app.
meta.migrationGuide: # Callout ‚Äî Carbon ‚Üí shadcn migration guide ## What `Callout` is Carbon's `Callout` (a.k.a. `StaticNotification`) is a **permanent, non-dismissible inline notification** ‚Äî meant for contextual messages that stay on the page (e.g., "Some changes cannot be undone" inside a settings panel). It's the "static" cousin of `ActionableNotification` and `InlineNotification`. shadcn ships exactly one primitive for inline notifications: **`Alert`**. The canonical source lives at `alert/alert.shadcn.tsx`; this wrapper re-exports it for migration ergonomics. ## Structural differences Carbon's `Callout` is **monolithic**: one component, prop-driven via `kind`, `title`, `subtitle`, `actionButtonLabel`, `onActionButtonClick`, `lowContrast`, `statusIconDescription`. shadcn's `Alert` is **decomposed**: assemble the icon, title, description, and optional CTA button yourself. | Concept | Carbon | shadcn | |---|---|---| | Wrapper | `` | `‚Ä¶` | | Icon | implicit (chosen from `kind`) | render manually as a child (e.g., `lucide-react` `` / ``) | | Title | `title` prop | `` | | Subtitle / body | `subtitle` prop | `` | | Action button | `actionButtonLabel` + `onActionButtonClick` | `` placed inside the Alert (typical Callout CTA is quiet, so `outline`/`ghost` matches better than `default`). 5. If `lowContrast` was set: add `className="border-transparent bg-muted"` to mute the visual weight. 6. Drop `statusIconDescription` unless your icon is non-decorative; add an `‚Ä¶` next to the icon if needed. 7. If multiple Callouts in the same view: consider extracting your status-mapped Alert into a tiny local helper (``, ``) or extend `alertVariants` with `info` / `warning` entries to centralise the styling.
